music.name: Pink Block Pride ft Honey Dijon
music.why: Real house pedigree in daylight — the connoisseur's Saturday, not the EDM block parties
music.summary: Daytime Pride house party at The Great Northern featuring DJ and producer Honey Dijon, kicking off at noon with rooftop and block-party vibes on Pride Saturday.

music.name: Perc (+ heavy bill)
music.why: The hardest, longest techno option of Pride; dirt-cheap and built for the all-nighter
music.summary: London industrial-techno titan Perc headlines a heavy all-night bill at F8 in SoMa for SF Pride, running from early afternoon through 3am with a supporting cast of hard techno acts.

music.name: Barbazon: Shygirl / Romy / Avalon Emerson / Pabllo Vittar / Vengaboys
music.why: The all-day-into-night Pride marathon for one ticket — most range from pop to underground without leaving
music.summary: Rhonda & Squish's five-act Pride blowout at The Great Northern: street block party 2–9pm with Shygirl, Romy, Avalon Emerson, Pabllo Vittar, and Vengaboys, then afterparty inside until 2am. 21+ only.

music.name: Kaskade
music.venue: Heart Health Park at Cal Expo
music.why: Crowd-pleasing melodic EDM outdoors; the gentler X Games opener for non-bassheads
music.summary: EDM veteran Kaskade opens the inaugural MoonPay X Games Sacramento concert series Friday night at Cal Expo's Heart Health Park, alongside three days of pro skateboard, BMX, and Moto X.

music.name: Subtronics + Mustard
music.why: Heavy bass plus action sports in one trip; worth the Sacramento drive if you want the spectacle
music.summary: Subtronics and Mustard co-headline Saturday night at MoonPay X Games Sacramento, the debut festival at Cal Expo blending elite action sports (skate/BMX/Moto X) with major EDM and hip-hop acts.

music.name: Zedd Pride Block Party
music.why: The big-budget Pride Sunday splurge; pay up for spectacle, not intimacy
music.summary: Zedd headlines The Midway's flagship Pride block party from 2–10pm outdoors in SoMa — the venue's biggest Pride Sunday draw at $125.

music.name: Joel Corry & James Kennedy
music.why: Cheap chart-house nightcap after the block party; come for the after, not the deep cuts
music.summary: Official after-party for the Zedd Pride Block Party inside The Midway, with British chart-house DJ Joel Corry and Vanderpump Rules personality DJ James Kennedy from 10pm–2am. 21+.

music.name: Planeta Pisces x Oscat: Planeta Perra - La Favi, Robertitx, Oscat, Digital KitKat, Villin, Daddy YankMe
music.why: Sweaty queer-Latin perreo for cheap — the Mission's answer to the big-room parties
music.summary: Six-DJ reggaeton, perreo, and latincore takeover at The Knockout SF with La Favi, Robertitx, Oscat, Digital KitKat, Villin, and Daddy YankMe spanning electroclash and darkwave. 9pm–2am.

music.name: Mark Knight
music.why: Headliner-grade tech-house at a fraction of the weekend's prices; the value pick
music.summary: Toolroom Records founder and tech-house don Mark Knight plays Audio SF on Pride Saturday — one of the most affordable headliner sets of the weekend at $22.

music.name: Aaron Hibell
music.address: 1300 Van Ness Avenue, San Francisco, CA 94109
music.why: Atmospheric, cinematic trance in SF's prettiest ballroom — for mood over mayhem
music.summary: Cinematic trance and techno artist Aaron Hibell performs at the grand Regency Ballroom on Pride Saturday, bringing atmospheric, sweeping electronic sets to SF's most ornate mid-size venue.

music.name: Bhaskar, Just Lucas
music.why: Tomorrowland-sized name at a dive-bar door price; the steal of Pride Friday
music.summary: Brazilian tech-house star Bhaskar — son of DJ parents, 500M streams on 'Infinity,' veteran of Burning Man and Tomorrowland — plays Madarae SF with Just Lucas b2b Jjkø and supporting acts. 9pm–3am, 21+.

music.name: Dillstradamus (Dillon Francis b2b Flosstradamus)
music.why: Festival-trap nostalgia in a seated theater; 18+, so the younger crowd gets in
music.summary: Dillon Francis and Flosstradamus unite as Dillstradamus at the Fox Theater Oakland (relocated from Bill Graham), with support from Ookay and Henry Fong. 18+, doors 7pm, show 8pm.

music.name: Eric Luttrell
music.why: Intimate melodic-house rooftop in Sacramento — small-room feeling for Anjunadeep devotees
music.summary: Anjunadeep artist Luttrell plays an intimate rooftop show at Darling Aviary Sacramento, presented by All Day All Night and Earth1 with support from Benjam, Scottie Scribbles, and Amicus. 5–10pm, 21+.

music.name: Ghost Dragon: Customer Appreciation Night
music.why: The rare free big-room bass night — headliner-grade dubstep with no cover, the South Bay's loophole on a Pride-weekend Saturday
music.summary: PURE Nightclub's Customer Appreciation Night in downtown Sunnyvale: free cover all night and $5 drinks before 11pm, headlined by Ghost Dragon with support from Cal1Panda and eFLi b2b ryl3r. 21+, music from 10pm to 2am.

music.name: L.P. Rhythm (US debut)
music.why: Catch a buzzy UK-house name on his US debut in Halcyon's dusk-to-dawn room — before the rooms get bigger
music.summary: L.P. Rhythm makes his US debut at Halcyon SF with 90s-rooted UK house, supported by Jeff Straw and Lefty. Friday night from 10pm in the SoMa dusk-to-dawn club.

music.name: Midsommar: Galantis, Claptone, Kungs
music.why: Daytime mainstage festival-house, wrapped by 10pm — for the polished crowd, not the late grime
music.summary: Outdoor Pride-weekend block party at The Midway with Swedish house duo Galantis, masked DJ Claptone, and French producer Kungs playing a three-act festival set from 3–10pm in SoMa.

music.name: Mighty Real ft Ultra Nate (live)
music.address: 10 S Van Ness Ave, San Francisco, CA 94103 (entrance @ 41 12th St)
music.why: Live vocals on a rooftop, not just DJ sets — the diva-house option for Pride Sunday
music.summary: Pride rooftop party at SVN West with Ultra Naté performing live alongside DJ sets from Tedd Patterson, Charles Hawthorne, and David Harness from 1–7pm. 21+.

music.name: PRIDE_INTRNCNL: Reinas, Coquettas, y Golosas
music.why: Queer-Latina sounds at a small Mission room — cheap and culturally rooted
music.summary: Neo-perreo, cumbia, baile-funk, and techno Pride night at Arcana Mission SF celebrating queer Latina sounds and culture from 9:30pm–1:30am.

music.name: Beep! Beep! Free Pride After Party ft Donald Glaude
music.why: Free post-parade landing spot — where you go when you don't want to pay to keep dancing
music.summary: HPZ's fourth annual free Pride parade after-party at Public Works starting at 2pm, with house legend Donald Glaude headlining a celebratory afternoon post-parade. No ticket needed.

music.name: Electroluxx Pride 2026
music.why: Festival-scale production at festival-scale prices — the all-out splurge if budget's no object
music.summary: High-production electronic Pride party at The Midway SF — the venue's premium Friday night show with festival-scale staging and production values. $141–232.

music.name: DAYFALL: Karizma, Colette, Mr. V
music.why: Grown-and-soulful rooftop at golden hour — the classy sunset move, no bass drops
music.summary: Sunset soulful-house event at The Pearl rooftop in SoMa with deep-house and disco-house selectors Karizma, Colette, and Mr. V playing from 6–10pm as daylight turns to dusk.

music.name: On The Catwalk: DJ Roby
music.why: Free dive-bar dancing on the far west side — neighborhood Pride away from the crush
music.summary: Free Pride Friday dance party at The Riptide dive bar on the far west end of SF, hosted by DJ Roby from 9:30pm–1:30am.

music.name: 55B + Jiji's Jazz Tour
music.why: Free genre-hopping jazz-meets-club set; the cultured Thursday for the curious
music.summary: JIJI's Jazz Club and Madrid Jazz Collective 55b bring their West Coast tour to Arcana SF — a genre-crossing set of jazz, house, D&B, funk, and disco from Jin Lee Baobei and TÍDA. Free, 21+, RSVP required, 9pm–midnight.

music.name: AFTERS: Epicenter (secret location)
music.why: The members-only secret-location afterhours for when the clubs close — DM-for-address, not a public door
music.summary: Invitation-only afterhours ('Afters Epicenter') at a secret SF location, 2–6am, with Mode Leeloo, Bob_E, Okoye, and Jonesy. Members only — DM the organizers for the address; Uber recommended.

music.name: Bearracuda SF: Beefcake Pride - Tony Maron, Mateo Segade
music.why: Bear-and-burly crowd, house all night — know the scene you're walking into
music.summary: Bearracuda's 'Beefcake Pride' night at Public Works SF with DJs Tony Maron and Mateo Segade spinning house for a bear and queer crowd on Pride Friday night. 9pm–3am.

music.name: Bored Lord
music.why: Tiny-room buzzy talent over big production — for those who want sweat and a tight booth
music.summary: Rising Brooklyn producer Bored Lord brings her blend of deep house, breaks, and UK garage to Bar Part Time in SoMa for a Pride Friday late-night set.

music.name: Brews Crew Free Bass Day Party
music.why: Free, queer, DIY-soundsystem bass in the East Bay — for heads who skip the SF crowds
music.summary: Flowstate Soundsystem's self-described 'gayest bass pride party in the bay' at Admiral Maltings in Alameda — Arktoi, Lucystoner, Tummy Gang, and more playing dubstep, 140, UKG, and leftfield bass 1–9pm on a boutique DIY soundsystem.

music.name: Club Slayyy
music.why: Pure hyperpop fan service — go if that's your scene, skip if you want anything subtle
music.summary: Slayyyter-inspired hyperpop and electropop night at The Starlet Room Sacramento — a high-energy club celebration of hyperpop culture. $17, 9:15pm.

music.name: DJs w/in X Enchi X Reakper
music.why: Gritty acid-jungle-ghettotech for the experimental crowd; cheap and femme-led
music.summary: Three-act Sacramento night billed as 'divine femininity' — Reakper on garage and industrial techno, Enchi on electro and acid, Within closing with jungle, ghetto-tech, and house. 21+, $13.

music.name: Eclypse Edm Haus
music.venue: Key Club Aka Lock And Key
music.why: Sacramento's reliable weekly riddim fix — dark, heavy, and cheap for the bass faithful
music.summary: Sacramento's weekly EDM haus night at Key Club — dubstep and riddim from MuttDub plus trance from Kosmikionic, with dark visuals and a full rave atmosphere. 21+, 10:30pm–1:30am.

music.name: Feral Fridays
music.why: South Bay's local bass crew throwing down cheap — skip the SF trek if you're down here
music.summary: Impulsive Vibes' monthly bass party at Tiki Pete San Jose — this edition celebrates a birthday with the best local DJs playing trap, house, dubstep, and bass music. 9pm–2am.

music.name: Fixed Gloss: Datamosh3r, Moonpie, Alice Stribling
music.why: Cheap, cheeky electroclash for the post-punk weirdos — not your standard house night
music.summary: Launch night of Fixed Gloss at Underground SF on Haight — cheeky electro, post-punk synth, and electroclash from Datamosh3r and Moonpie (She's in Parties residents), hosted by Alice Stribling. $10 at the door, 9pm–late.

music.name: Flat Line: L.Kay, Vegapunk, Powell Ranger, Kosmikonic
music.why: Pure underground dubstep with no frills; cheap and heavy for Sacramento bassheads
music.summary: Four-act dubstep and bass night at Reunion Underground Sacramento with L.Kay, Vegapunk, Powell Ranger, and Kosmikonic covering dubstep, bass-music, trap, and hard techno. 9pm–2am.

music.name: I Feel Scene: Technopagan, Queermom, DJ Saratonin, Estellita, DJ Bunny Bexxx
music.why: Secret-spot queer chaos with vendors and a cuddle puddle — community over club polish
music.summary: Secret-location queer Oakland Pride party with hardcore, happy-hardcore, jersey-club, and hyperpop from Technopagan, Queermom, DJ Saratonin, and Estellita. Community craft space, vendors, food, and cuddle puddle. 21+, 8pm–late.

music.name: Leisure: Pride Kick Off & Tqid Anniversary
music.why: Low-cost, low-stakes way to open Pride weekend — the easy first stop
music.summary: Pride kickoff party and Tqid anniversary celebration at Cat Club SF on the first night of Pride weekend, with electronic music and club vibes for $10.

music.name: Meltdown w/ Cheyenne Giles, Iku, Shyy, 1kcuts, Dvan, momo
music.why: Stacked late-running bass bill at mid price — the deep-roster night for value hunters
music.summary: Mach8 Industries presents Meltdown at The Foundry SF — a six-act electronic lineup headlined by Cheyenne Giles, with Iku, Shyy, 1kcuts, Dvan, and momo. 9pm–2:45am, $28.

music.name: Mostly Cloudy Pride: Amarji King, Byrell The Great, felipe d, QUEENIE, Just Another Bitch, Discnogirl, GIRLS ON DECK, Afakasi Papi
music.why: Footwork and ballroom heat for the cognoscenti — not your average Pride house night
music.summary: Eight-act juke, footwork, and club-music Pride showcase at Monarch SF featuring Amarji King, Byrell The Great, felipe d, QUEENIE, Just Another Bitch, Discnogirl, GIRLS ON DECK, and Afakasi Papi. 9pm–3am.

music.name: North Beach Nights: Live at The Legacy
music.why: A free house-and-techno residency in a tiny North Beach bar — neighborhood underground, not a club ticket
music.summary: North Beach Nights residency at The Legacy on Grant Ave: free-entry house and techno from Yango, Mode Leeloo, and Silent J, 9pm–midnight (open later if the crowd's good). Runs every 3rd Thursday.

music.name: NYX Late Night (Saturday)
music.why: The later, deeper NYX night — three DJs in rotation past 1am, address to subscribers only
music.summary: NYX Late Night Saturday session from 1am with three rotating DJs. Location shared to NYX's RSVP list; phones-down, no-filming late-night series.

music.name: NYX Late Night ft. ESEJOHNP
music.why: A phones-down after-2am session for when the clubs shut — location shared only to NYX's RSVP list
music.summary: NYX Late Night Friday session from 2am headlined by ESEJOHNP. Location revealed to subscribers (sign up via the NYX bio link); strict no-filming, late-night clubbing series.

music.name: Slurrp: Pride - Sequoixa, Galvanize, Giuliano
music.why: Costume-forward Castro bounce party; come dressed cosmic-glam or feel underdressed
music.summary: Slurrp celebrates its first anniversary at Q Bar in the Castro with high-energy house, techno, and pop from Sequoixa, Galvanize, and headliner Giuliano (Trade, Bounce House, BAAAHS). Cosmic-glam attire encouraged, 9pm–2am.

music.name: SOMA Nights - PRIDE Edition feat. Jacki, My My, Cali Rose, & Mr. S Leather
music.why: Free early-evening queer warm-up in the leather district — start here before the night ramps
music.summary: Sweat.mp3's queer-led SOMA Nights Pride edition at 1145 Folsom — four hours of global club music from Jacki, MyMy, and Cali Rose in the heart of SoMa's leather district. Free, 5–9pm.

music.name: Soulful House Music Thursdays w/ DJ Lillyfield
music.why: Free low-key neighborhood pregame — the warm Thursday before the weekend goes loud
music.summary: DJ Lillyfield's weekly Thursday residency at The Sea Star in Dogpatch starts with Motown and soul at 7pm then evolves into soulful house until 10pm — a warm neighborhood warm-up before Pride weekend.

music.name: Stormy Deep Nights - Night01
music.why: Lo-fi, jazzy, talk-friendly vibe — for a chill night out, not a dancefloor sweat
music.summary: Launch night of Stormy Deep Nights at Mr. Mahjong's SF — a lo-fi, NuJazz, TrapSoul, and alternative R&B room focused on vibe and atmosphere. Free, 8pm.

music.name: TouchGrass4 @ Dolores Park
music.why: Free, no ticket, all-day park hang — the casual call vs the paid bass parties
music.summary: Free outdoor dubstep and bass party in Mission Dolores Park running 11am–7pm, the fourth edition of the TouchGrass free all-day event series.

sports.name: Bicycle Film Festival
sports.why: A sit-down, indoor break for bike culture — the low-key counter to the dancefloors
sports.summary: The Bicycle Film Festival screens bike culture short films and features at Gray Area Theater on Mission Street, Saturday at 3pm. $22.

sports.name: Different Spokes Pride Ride 2026 (Pink Triangle Tour)
sports.address: Marx Meadow, JFK Dr & 25th Ave, San Francisco, CA 94121
sports.why: Free queer group ride through Pride landmarks — the social, sightseeing way to mark the day
sports.summary: Free group bike ride by Different Spokes cycling club, departing Marx Meadow in Golden Gate Park at 9:15am on a Pink Triangle Tour route through SF Pride landmarks.

sports.name: Dykes on Bikes Women's Motorcycle Contingent @ SF Pride
sports.address: Market St & Steuart St, San Francisco, CA 94105
sports.why: Be in the parade, not watching it — register early, this one's free to ride
sports.summary: The iconic Dykes on Bikes Women's Motorcycle Contingent leads off the SF Pride Parade, staging at Market & Steuart from 8–10am. Registration required on Eventbrite; free to participate.

sports.name: Full Moon Kayak Tour with Sea Trek
sports.why: A serene full-moon paddle far from the Pride crowds — the quiet, splurge-worthy escape
sports.summary: Evening guided kayak tour on San Francisco Bay under the full moon, departing from Sea Trek's base at Schoonmaker Beach in Sausalito. $90–110 per person.

sports.name: Great Highway / Lands End 10K
sports.venue: Taraval Street & Great Highway (Ocean Beach), start/finish
sports.address: Taraval Street and Great Highway, San Francisco, CA 94116
sports.why: Cheapest organized race around, on a flat oceanfront course — racing without the entry-fee sting
sports.summary: DSE Runners' flat 10K starting at Ocean Beach (Taraval & Great Highway), running north to Lands End and back. Check-in 8am, gun 9am. One of the cheapest organized races in SF at $5–10.

sports.name: Mare Island Half Marathon
sports.venue: Mare Island Brewing Co. – Coal Sheds
sports.why: Flat historic-shipyard route that ends at a taproom — the reward-at-the-finish race
sports.summary: Half marathon (plus 10K and 5K options) through the historic shipyard and waterfront of Mare Island in Vallejo, with a 7:30am start and a finish at Mare Island Brewing Co.'s Coal Sheds taproom.

sports.name: Pride House SF x Queer & Well: Yoga with Cyrus
sports.why: Free grounding reset before the chaos — the antidote to a weekend of late nights
sports.summary: Free outdoor yoga class with instructor Cyrus hosted at Pride House SF in Mid-Market — a grounding way to start Pride weekend before the parties begin.

sports.name: Rock the Loch (5K/10K/Half trail)
sports.address: 17930 Lake Chabot Rd, Castro Valley, CA 94546
sports.why: Lakeside trails with a distance for every level — bring beginners and hardcores alike
sports.summary: Multi-distance trail race (5K/10K/half marathon) at Lake Chabot Regional Park in Castro Valley, looping the lush reservoir trails. Pick your distance, $59–107.

sports.name: SF Critical Mass
sports.why: Free, leaderless street takeover on two wheels — show up and ride, no plan needed
sports.summary: SF Critical Mass's last-Friday-of-the-month bike ride departs Embarcadero Plaza at 5:30pm — this edition falls on Pride Friday, making it an especially festive mass cycling takeover of city streets.

sports.name: SF FrontRunners Pride Run (5K/10K)
sports.address: Middle Dr W & Metson Rd, Golden Gate Park, San Francisco, CA 94122
sports.why: Charity run that doubles as your Pride morning warm-up — sweat for a cause first
sports.summary: SF FrontRunners' annual Pride Run through Golden Gate Park offered as a 5K or 10K, benefiting LGBTQ+ athletic communities. 9am start from Middle Dr W & Metson Rd.

sports.name: Sunset Dunes parkrun 5K (free)
sports.address: 1349 Upper Great Hwy, San Francisco, CA 94122
sports.why: Free timed 5K by the ocean — the no-pressure morning option, just register and show
sports.summary: Free timed weekly 5K parkrun on the car-free Upper Great Highway in SF's Outer Sunset, starting at 9am Saturday. Register at parkrun.us/sunsetdunes/ before showing up.

sports.name: That Dam Race @ Calero Reservoir
sports.why: Remote South Bay backcountry trail — for runners who want wilderness, not a city course
sports.summary: Trail half-marathon and marathon through wild Calero Reservoir backcountry in the Santa Cruz foothills, starting from Calero County Park in South San Jose.

sports.name: Woodmonster Trail Race 10M
sports.why: A genuinely tough redwood climb for trail runners chasing elevation, not a flat PR
sports.summary: Ten-mile trail race through the Oakland Hills starting at Chabot Space & Science Center on Skyline Blvd, covering rugged single-track through redwood and oak forest.
